title: Rich Vietnamese Hind Shank Bo Kho
description: A savory and shattering Vietnamese dish featuring hind shank, served in an agave leaf vessel, perfect for a brunch mood. This no-onion-no-garlic-friendly recipe is a variation of the traditional bo kho.
instructions: In a large pot, combine the hind shank, beef broth, fish sauce, lemongrass, ginger, and star anise, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Add the pasta water and tomato paste to the broth and whisk to combine.
Bring the broth to a simmer and cook until it has thickened slightly.
To serve, place a portion of the hind shank onto an agave leaf, spoon some of the broth over the top, and serve immediately.
Garnish with fresh herbs, such as cilantro or basil, if desired.
